name: Coffea arabica var. typica
description: A classic Jamaican Blue Mountain-style coffee with a mild, well-balanced flavour, gentle acidity, and a clean, sweet finish carrying subtle notes of chocolate and nuts.

name: Coffea arabica var. bourbon
description: Grown in the high-altitude hills of Rwanda, this Bourbon variety offers a juicy body with bright red-fruit acidity and delicate floral and caramel undertones.

name: Coffea canephora (robusta)
description: A bold, full-bodied Vietnamese Robusta with low acidity, intense bitterness, and earthy dark-chocolate notes. High in caffeine and ideal for strong espresso blends.

name: Coffea arabica 'Geisha'
description: A rare and prized Geisha celebrated for its complex, tea-like cup, vivid jasmine aroma, and layered notes of bergamot, peach, and tropical fruit.

name: Coffea arabica var. caturra
description: A smooth Colombian Caturra with medium body, rounded sweetness, and balanced notes of caramel, red apple, and a hint of citrus.

name: Coffea arabica var. catuai
description: A dependable Brazilian Catuai offering a creamy body, low acidity, and comforting flavours of milk chocolate, hazelnut, and roasted almond.

name: Coffea arabica 'SL28'
description: A vibrant Kenyan SL28 known for its juicy blackcurrant acidity, syrupy body, and bright, wine-like complexity with a lingering sweet finish.

name: Coffea arabica 'Heirloom'
description: Wild heirloom varieties from the birthplace of coffee, delivering a fragrant floral aroma, delicate berry sweetness, and a light, tea-like body.

name: Coffea liberica
description: An unusual Liberica (Barako) with a heavy body, smoky and woody character, and distinctive notes of jackfruit, dark fruit, and a bittersweet finish.

name: Coffea arabica 'Pacamara'
description: A large-bean Salvadoran Pacamara prized for its creamy texture, bright stone-fruit acidity, and complex notes of chocolate, citrus, and brown sugar.

name: Coffea arabica var. mundo novo
description: A robust Brazilian Mundo Novo with a heavy body, gentle acidity, and rich, full flavours of dark chocolate, toasted nuts, and molasses.

name: Coffea arabica 'Maragogipe'
description: Known as the 'elephant bean', this Mexican Maragogipe is mild and smooth with light acidity, a soft body, and subtle notes of cocoa and toasted grain.

name: Coffea arabica var. castillo
description: A disease-resistant Colombian Castillo supplied as unroasted green beans for home roasters. Expect a balanced cup with medium body, mild acidity, and sweet caramel and red-fruit notes once roasted.

name: Coffea canephora var. robusta
description: Unroasted green Ugandan Robusta beans for home roasting, full-bodied with earthy, nutty character and a powerful caffeine kick once roasted.

name: Coffea excelsa
description: A rare Indonesian Excelsa with a tart, fruity profile, light body, and intriguing notes of dark berries, spice, and a faintly smoky finish.

name: Coffea arabica 'Tabi'
description: A bright Costa Rican Tabi with crisp citrus acidity, a clean medium body, and sweet notes of honey, orange, and red apple.
